concept: Interpret / Convey Meaning Between Languages or Communication Modes
description: To convey meaning between languages or communication modes for an interaction, with the source expression and the new expression remaining distinct. Interpretation may be simultaneous, consecutive, signed, spoken, tactile, or otherwise specified. 'shorela' (rephrase) changes wording within an exchange; 'shoraku' crosses a language or mode boundary.
sound_symbolism: Soft 'sho' begins in expressed language, while open 'ra' gives meaning room to cross. Final 'ku' gathers the conveyed expression into a form another participant can receive.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'shia ha phelui shoraku.' (They interpret these words.) Source and receiving languages or modes can appear in surrounding phrases. The event-noun rule supplies interpretation, while an interpreting person remains 'miona' with an applicable role rather than a new person class.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Interpretation can open dialogue across languages without granting the interpreter ownership of the speaker's view or authority to decide for them.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Multilingual traders, travelers, and neighboring communities relied on people who could carry meaning across speech traditions while every retelling still required trust and correction.

tags.cognition: understanding one expression well enough to convey its meaning
tags.dialogue: carrying meaning between languages or communication modes
tags.ritual: interpretation where exact communal or ceremonial wording matters
